CREATE TABLE test_data (id INTEGER, value INTEGER);
SELECT bit_or(value) FROM test_data WHERE id > 2;
SELECT id, bit_or(value) FROM test_data GROUP BY id;
CREATE TABLE other_data (id INTEGER, flag INTEGER);
SELECT a.id, bit_or(a.value) FROM test_data a JOIN other_data b ON a.id = b.id GROUP BY a.id;
SELECT id, bit_or(value) OVER (PARTITION BY id) FROM test_data;
SELECT bit_or(subquery.value) FROM (SELECT value FROM test_data WHERE value % 2 = 0) AS subquery;
SELECT bit_or(a.value) FROM (SELECT value FROM test_data WHERE value IS NOT NULL) AS a;
SELECT bit_or(value) FROM (SELECT value FROM test_data ORDER BY value LIMIT 10) AS subquery;
CREATE TABLE complex_data (id INTEGER, val1 INTEGER, val2 INTEGER);
SELECT id, bit_or(val1) | bit_or(val2) FROM complex_data GROUP BY id;
SELECT id, bit_or(val1) FROM complex_data WHERE val2 > 5 GROUP BY id;
SELECT DISTINCT id, bit_or(val1) FROM (SELECT * FROM complex_data UNION SELECT * FROM test_data) AS combined GROUP BY id;
SELECT COUNT(*), bit_or(value) FROM test_data WHERE value < 10 GROUP BY value HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
